competitor_patterns: Extreme depth: Bikerumor's #1-ranking post has 24,389 words and covers 18+ tested products with tables, FAQs, and structured H2s per product — our posts average 2,000–3,400 words, far below this bar for competitive head terms

Testing authority signals: Top competitors (Outdoorgearlab, Bikerumor) lead with 'We tested X models' language and named reviewer bylines, establishing real-world credibility that AI overviews and Google trust more than brand-written content

Comparison tables are near-universal: Bikerumor, 220Triathlon, and Outdoorgearlab all use structured comparison tables (weight, lens type, price, UV rating) — these win featured snippets and table rich results for 'best cycling glasses' queries

Frequent freshness updates: Bikerumor updated their guide on May 21, 2026 (8 days ago) with named new additions in the snippet itself — Google's snippet explicitly shows the update date, which boosts CTR for time-sensitive 'best of 2026' queries

Segmented sub-categories within one post: Outdoorgearlab uses H2s like 'Best MTB Sunglasses', 'Best Road Bike Glasses', 'Best Value', 'Best Budget' — one URL captures multiple long-tail intents simultaneously, multiplying ranking surface area
our_gaps: Word count is 6–10x below top competitors on head terms: our 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026' post is 2,690 words vs Bikerumor's 24,389 — Google ranks depth for this query type; we cannot win position 1–3 without at least 6,000–8,000 words of genuinely useful content

No comparison tables in our main buying guide ('Best Cycling Glasses 2026' post has has_table: false) — this means we miss featured snippet eligibility for queries like 'cycling glasses comparison' and 'best cycling glasses weight chart'

Zero third-party or multi-product reviews: all our posts are Velluto-centric; competitors rank by reviewing 10–18 products and positioning their picks objectively — without covering alternatives honestly, we cannot earn trust signals for editorial 'best of' queries or AI citation

No named author or testing methodology section: top competitors explicitly state who tested the product and how ('Review by Justin Simoni', 'We tested on real rides') — our posts lack this, reducing E-E-A-T signals Google requires for YMYL-adjacent product advice content

Event-based content (Amstel Gold Race post, 1,125 words) is too thin and too narrow to sustain traffic beyond the event window — these posts need either evergreen reframing or much greater depth to justify crawl budget and indexing
keyword_opportunities: best interchangeable lens cycling glasses 2026 — Demonsun ranks top 3 for this; our lens-swap system (VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione, tool-free click-in) is a direct product match and we have zero dedicated content targeting this exact phrase

lightweight cycling glasses under 30g — no competitor has a post specifically targeting sub-30g weight as the primary angle; our 25g frame is a concrete, verifiable differentiator that can own this query with a dedicated post

cycling glasses anti fog road cycling climbs — 220Triathlon and Bikerumor touch on fog but have no dedicated long-form content; our existing anti-fog post (3,357 words) is our strongest asset but needs internal linking and schema markup to surface for this variant

cycling glasses 30 day trial free return — zero competitors offer or target this purchase-decision query; this is a pure bottom-of-funnel keyword where Velluto has a unique offer and no content competition

best budget cycling glasses with UV400 — 220Triathlon targets 'best cheap cycling glasses' and ranks well; Velluto's Italian-design-at-fraction-of-premium-price positioning maps directly to this intent gap and we have no post addressing price-conscious buyers explicitly
next_post_guidelines: Write at minimum 5,500 words for any post targeting 'best cycling glasses', 'road cycling glasses', or 'interchangeable lens glasses' — use Bikerumor's structure as the floor, not the ceiling; include a TL;DR table at the top, per-section H2s, and a buying criteria section

Add a structured HTML comparison table to every buying guide and review post: columns must include Weight (g), Lens Type, Interchangeable (Y/N), Anti-Fog (Y/N), UV400 (Y/N), Price (€), and Trial Period — this directly targets table rich results and comparison featured snippets

Open every post with a first-person testing hook in the first 50 words: e.g. 'We rode the StradaPro across 400km of road and gravel to test this claim' — this mimics the E-E-A-T pattern that earns Outdoorgearlab and Bikerumor their authority rankings

Include a 'Who This Is For / Who Should Look Elsewhere' section in every review — this honest framing signals editorial objectivity to Google's quality raters and increases time-on-page by matching user intent precisely before they bounce

Target sub-category H2s within each major buying guide post (e.g. 'Best Cycling Glasses for Road', 'Best for Gravel', 'Best Lightweight Option', 'Best Value Under €100') — each H2 captures a distinct long-tail query and multiplies the post's ranking surface within one URL

Publish or update buying guide posts with a visible 'Last updated: [date]' tag in the HTML and meta snippet — Bikerumor's May 21 update is explicitly shown in their Google snippet and demonstrably drives higher CTR for time-sensitive '2026' queries

Add FAQ schema with at least 5 questions per post, focusing on questions that mirror 'People Also Ask' patterns: e.g. 'Are interchangeable lens cycling glasses worth it?', 'What weight should cycling glasses be?', 'Do anti-fog coatings wash off?' — these directly feed Google AI Overviews and PAA boxes
seo_quick_wins: Add a comparison table (HTML table, not image) to every existing post that currently lacks one — especially 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026' (has_table: false) — retrofit with a 6-column table comparing StradaPro against 3–4 named competitors on weight, lens system, anti-fog, UV400, price, and trial policy; this can be done without rewriting the full post and immediately improves featured snippet eligibility

Add Article and FAQPage structured data (JSON-LD schema) to all existing posts — currently our posts have has_faq: true in content but schema markup status is unconfirmed; implementing FAQPage schema on the anti-fog and best cycling glasses posts specifically can trigger PAA box appearances within days of reindexing

Strengthen internal linking: create a hub-and-spoke structure where 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026' links to every topic-specific post (anti-fog, interchangeable lenses, lightweight, polarised) with exact-match anchor text — this concentrates PageRank on the head-term post and signals topical authority to Googlebot across the cluster
meta_title_fixes: No GSC data available — cannot identify low-CTR queries from real impressions. Action required: connect Google Search Console to velluto-shop.com and return data after 28-day accumulation period (approximately 2026-06-26) to generate data-driven meta title fix recommendations. In the interim, apply this heuristic to all existing posts: ensure every meta title includes the year '2026', a concrete benefit (e.g. '25g', 'Anti-Fog', 'Interchangeable Lenses'), and stays under 60 characters — e.g. 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: 25g & Anti-Fog | Velluto'
content_quick_wins: No GSC position data available yet — cannot identify near-top-3 keywords from real ranking data. Action required: after GSC data accumulates (target date: 2026-06-26), re-run this analysis to surface specific queries ranking positions 4–15 where content additions (tables, FAQ schema, word count expansion) can push rankings into top 3. Priority candidates based on existing post topics: 'anti fog cycling sunglasses' (post exists, 3,357 words — likely indexing now), 'best cycling glasses 2026' (post exists, 2,690 words — needs expansion to 6,000+ words to be competitive)
geo_angles: Position Velluto StradaPro as the answer to 'What is the lightest cycling glass with interchangeable lenses?' — create a dedicated post or FAQ block with this exact phrasing, state '25g — lighter than Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour, and Tifosi Rail' with cited weight sources; AI systems like Perplexity and ChatGPT pull specific factual comparisons with named competitors when answering product recommendation queries

Create a 'Velluto StradaPro: Full Specifications & Independent Test Summary' page with structured data (Product schema: weight, material, lens options, UV rating, price, trial period) — Google AI Overviews and Perplexity preferentially cite pages with complete, machine-readable product specifications when answering 'best cycling glasses' queries in conversational search

Publish a 'Why 30-Day Risk-Free Trials Change How You Buy Cycling Glasses' thought-leadership post that frames Velluto's trial policy as a category innovation — AI answer engines frequently cite content that explains 'why' something matters rather than just 'what' it is, and no competitor currently holds authority on trial-based cycling eyewear purchasing as a topic
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Bikerumor — angle: 'What Bikerumor's Best Cycling Sunglasses Guide Doesn't Test' | Headline: 'We Rode 400km in the Glasses Bikerumor Missed in 2026' | Key differentiator: Bikerumor tests established brands (Julbo, Oakley, POC, Tifosi) — no independent Italian-designed ultralight option at Velluto's price point was included; lead with 25g weight advantage and 30-day trial as the proof point that removes the risk of trying an unlisted brand

COMPETITOR: Outdoorgearlab — angle: 'Best Cycling Sunglasses for Road: How Velluto StradaPro Compares to Outdoorgearlab's Top Picks' | Headline: 'Oakley vs Roka vs Velluto StradaPro: The Weight & Lens System Test' | Key differentiator: Outdoorgearlab ranks products by test score but does not cover tool-free interchangeable lens systems at sub-€150 price points; lead with VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione dual-lens system vs single-lens designs in changing light conditions

COMPETITOR: Demonsun (ranks top 3 for interchangeable lens cycling glasses) — angle: 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses: Velluto StradaPro vs Demonsun — Which System Actually Works?' | Headline: 'Click-In Lenses vs Screw-System: The Real-World Interchangeable Lens Test' | Key differentiator: Velluto's tool-free click-in system vs Demonsun's mechanism; lead with the practical use case of changing lenses mid-ride or in a cafe stop without tools, combined with anti-fog integration that Demonsun lacks
